training monotony strain Foster overtraining athletes

overtraining syndrome symptoms diagnosis athletes systematic review 2022 2023
Halson 2014 overtraining monitoring recovery endurance athletes Sports Medicine
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across detection/diagnosis (Carrard 2022 scoping review, Buyse 2019, Weakley 2022 systematic review), monitoring tools (Foster 1998 session-RPE, Halson 2014 training load, Kellmann 2010 stress-recovery, Lundstrom 2023 HRV), pathophysiology (Armstrong 2022 complex systems, Cadegiani 2019 EROS study), practical clinical guides (Kreher 2012), and foundational overreaching research (Lehmann 1991 prospective). Excludes Meeusen 2013 consensus statement as directed. Gap: limited running-specific RCTs inducing OTS (ethically difficult). All study types except meta-analysis represented (no true meta-analysis exists for OTS diagnosis). Halson & Jeukendrup 2004 provides critical analysis of whether OTS truly exists in research literature.

papers.title: Diagnosing Overtraining Syndrome: A Scoping Review
papers.notes: Scoping review of 39 studies evaluating diagnostic markers and scoring systems for overtraining syndrome.

papers.title: Overtraining Syndrome Symptoms and Diagnosis in Athletes: Where Is the Research? A Systematic Review
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Systematic review finding zero studies providing objective evidence of detailed performance changes from prior to OTS diagnosis.

papers.title: Overtraining Syndrome: A Practical Guide
papers.notes: Clinical review of OTS pathophysiology hypotheses (glycogen, serotonin, inflammation), diagnosis as exclusion, and practical prevention strategies.

papers.title: Overtraining Syndrome as a Complex Systems Phenomenon
papers.notes: Proposed a complex systems framework for OTS, advocating trans-omics and machine learning over single-biomarker approaches.

papers.title: Improving the Diagnosis of Nonfunctional Overreaching and Overtraining Syndrome
papers.notes: Used discriminant analysis on hormonal responses during two-bout exercise protocol to differentiate NFO from OTS with 98% sensitivity.

papers.title: Monitoring training in athletes with reference to overtraining syndrome
papers.notes: Introduced session-RPE method and training monotony/strain calculations, showing illness correlated with individually identifiable training thresholds.

papers.title: Monitoring training load to understand fatigue in athletes
papers.notes: Reviewed external and internal training load monitoring methods and their practical implementation for preventing non-functional overreaching.

papers.title: Preventing overtraining in athletes in high-intensity sports and stress/recovery monitoring
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
papers.notes: Reviewed stress-recovery monitoring instruments and their role in balancing training load with recovery to prevent overtraining.

papers.title: Novel insights of overtraining syndrome discovered from the EROS study
papers.notes: Compared 67 clinical and hormonal parameters across OTS athletes, healthy athletes, and sedentary controls, identifying 'paradoxical deconditioning' as a key mechanism.

papers.title: Training-overtraining. A prospective, experimental study with experienced middle- and long-distance runners
papers.notes: Prospective study doubling training volume (86 to 175 km/week) in experienced runners to induce overtraining, monitoring cardiovascular, metabolic, and hormonal parameters.
